regions.comment: screen line LUT - low byte for each of 22 char rows (MODE 5, base &5800, stride &140)
screen line LUT - high byte (corresponds to L1209 entries)

Lives counters (player_hp, lives_left) + first three of the six per-hazard-slot state arrays (hazard_x[10], hazard_y[9], hazard_type[9]). All are pure data — the tracer otherwise mis-decodes them as `ASL zp_06` / BRK / NOP because the initial bytes happen to be valid opcodes. The remaining three arrays (hazard_flip / _hp / _step) are already in the synthesised data region at &206E..&208A.

Enemy slot state: enemy_x[11] + enemy_y[11] + enemy_state[11] + enemy_pattern_step[11] + enemy_pattern_id[10] (= 54 bytes covering &1A55..&1A8A). Active slots are indices 1..n_enemies (1..8); slots 0, 9, 10 are always zero. The tracer would otherwise decode these bytes as NOP/BRK (because the zero-init bytes happen to be valid opcodes — &EA = NOP, &00 = BRK).

Per-state animation-frame sprite-pointer LUT (parallel LSB+HSB tables, 7 entries each, indexed by enemy_state in [0..6]). Read by enemy_anim_dispatch (L1BE3). Slot 0 = &EAEA (placeholder; state 0 = inactive, never plotted). Slots 1..4 = lev_enemy_0..3 (the four 4×24 small-enemy frames in LEVD1). Slots 5..6 = gfx_enemy_small_frame0/1 in GRAPHIX (two 4×24 frames of a winged creature). Earlier GRAPHIX cfg labels mis-split this region into gfx_pad/gfx_bomb/3×24-frames; corrected in Session 22 to two contiguous 4×24 sprites at &4840 and &48A0, exactly back-to-back to the IRQ handler at &4900.

comments.0x1100: Entry: restore saved score, run per-level init, draw the title screen, then drop into main_loop. Called as `CALL &1100` from BASIC Runner/Loader4 on each level start.
comments.0x1141: LFSR pseudo-random generator. Runs 32 rounds of `LSR / EOR zp_11 / ROR / ROL` across the 5-byte state at zp_0D..zp_11 (Galois-style 40-bit LFSR — the bit dropped each round is fed back through zp_11). On return, four progressively right-shifted slices of the result byte are stored so callers can pick a bin size without further shifting: rndval256 (0..255), rndval64 (0..63 = raw>>2), rndval32 (0..31 = raw>>3), rndval16 (0..15 = raw>>4). Used heavily by the starfield and force-field renderers.
comments.0x1173: Plot a column-major sprite. Inputs: X = width in byte-cols, Y = height in scanlines, sprite_src_lo / sprite_src_hi (self-modified LDA at sprite_plot_lda_sm) = source bytes, zp_dest_x_lo / zp_dest_x_hi = destination screen address (typically set up by calc_screen_addr), zp_sprite_dir_flag = direction (1 = forward, 0 = vertical-flip — reads source bytes back-to-front).
comments.0x116F: Convenience wrapper: plot a sprite with hard-coded width = 1 byte-col and height = &20 scanlines, then fall into sprite_plot_xy.
comments.0x1209: Screen char-row low-byte LUT — MODE 5 with base &5800, stride &140 per char row
comments.0x121F: Screen char-row high-byte LUT — pairs with screen_row_lo_lut
comments.0x1236: Compute screen address from X (char-col) and Y (logical row); result in $76/$77
comments.0x14D9: Read input: if joystick mode (zp_9F == 1) take read_joystick path; else scan KEY_RIGHT / KEY_LEFT / KEY_DOWN / KEY_UP / KEY_FIRE in turn via check_key_pressed and dispatch the matching move_player_* / on_fire_pressed routine.
comments.0x1517: Read joystick: OSBYTE &80 reads analog joystick 1 X/Y axes (low/high thresholds &40/&C8) and the fire button bit, dispatching the same move_player_* / on_fire_pressed routines as the keyboard path.
comments.0x155B: Wraps OSBYTE &81 (INKEY -X) for negative key scan. Returns Z=1 when the key whose internal code is in X is NOT pressed (Y was &FF), Z=0 when pressed. Callers use BNE = not pressed / BEQ = pressed.
comments.0x1565: Move player one column LEFT (DEC zp_player_x, clamped at min &02).
comments.0x16A6: Move player one column RIGHT (INC zp_player_x, clamped at max &14).
comments.0x16B0: Move player one row DOWN (zp_player_y += 4, clamped at max &DC).
comments.0x16CC: Move player one row UP (zp_player_y -= 4, clamped at min &98).
comments.0x17B9: Fire button handler: if cooldown (zp_8A) elapsed, find the first empty player_bullet_x slot (search 4..1), store player_x+2 / player_y-&0C into it, reload cooldown from fire_cooldown_reload (init = 6), play sfx_fire.
comments.0x17E7: Update player bullets: iterate the 6 player_bullet slots; for each active one (player_bullet_x != 0) move +2 px right, erase + clear if it has crossed col &25 (off-screen right), else replot the 3x2 bullet sprite and run check_bullet_hits. Finally decrement zp_8A (the fire cooldown).
comments.0x1847: Check what the current player bullet has hit. Two loops over the same bullet position (zp_test_x = player_bullet_x+2, zp_test_y = player_bullet_y): (a) X=8..1 over the enemy slots in enemy_x/enemy_y/enemy_state — a hit INCs enemy_state[X] and at values 3/5/7 plays OSWRCH 7 + INCs pickup_kill_count; at >=9 the enemy is fully expired; (b) X=0..7 over the hazard slots — a hit decrements hazard_hp, plays explode, and on kill clears the slot. Either way, the bullet erases itself (3x2 brush) and the slot is freed.
comments.0x1AEB: Per-frame enemy update: iterate the 8 enemy slots (X=8..1 in enemy_state), move each by ±1 X / ±4 Y depending on data_16F6/16F7 direction bits, erase + redraw at the new position with a 4x24 sprite. Slot deactivates (and is erased) when enemy_state drops to 0. Enemies damage the player on contact (check_player_collisions) and are destructible by player bullets (check_bullet_hits).
comments.0x1F8E: Per-level init. Sets player start position (&05, &C8), zero-fills the six 9-slot hazard state tables (hazard_x, hazard_y, hazard_type, hazard_hp, hazard_step, enemy_state), clears single-byte state (player bullets, force-pod registers), sets fire_cooldown_reload=6, and marks all 7 hazard_bullet_x slots inactive (&FF).
comments.0x1478: Draw the player ship: plot lev_player_sprite (6×22 from &4E80 in LEVD1) at (zp_player_x, zp_player_y). If pod_attached == 1 (force-pod power-up active), also call draw_player_pod at (player_x+5, player_y+1). Then JSR check_player_collisions and JMP to update_player_bullets.
comments.0x14B5: Draw the player's force-pod (the rotating saucer that orbits the ship after a power-up). Selects one of three frames at gfx_pod_frame0/1/2 (&3900/&3960/&39C0) based on pod_anim_frame (= data_14D8, cycles 1/2/3 driven by vertical movement: move_player_down does INC, move_player_up does DEC, both wrap 1<->3). Falls through into sprite_plot_xy with X=4 byte-cols, Y=&18 lines.
comments.0x198B: Per-frame player collision check. Two loops: (a) X=8..1 over the enemy slots at enemy_x/_y/_state — for any slot whose enemy_state is in 1..9 (active), test the player against a 6×&18 bounding box; on hit, mark the slot &0A (expired) and JSR lose_a_life. (b) X=0..7 over the hazard slots (hazard_x, hazard_y, hazard_type < &14) — same test. Each hit triggers a `OSWRCH &07` (the BBC bell, redefined by Loader2 lines 5-9 via `*FX211..214` to be a short non-musical blip used as the in-game collision sound).
comments.0x1E47: Player death animation. Plays sfx_explode then plots 6 frames of a 4×32 explosion sprite (`lev_explosion_0..5`) at the player position with `frame_delay` between frames. The source pointer pairs live in slots 21..26 of `lev_hazard_ptr_*` (aliased as `lev_explosion_ptr_lo` / `_hi` at &7A95 / &7AD5). Frames 0..3 are in LEVD1 (`lev_explosion_0..3` at &4A00 / &4A80 / &4B00 / &4B80); frames 4..5 are in LEVD2 (`lev_explosion_4..5` at &7C00 / &7C80). After the last frame, DEC `lives_left` and redraw the lives-remaining indicator at row &57; if any lives are left, also plays intro_get_ready and re-runs init to start the next ship.
comments.0x1DEE: Per-hit handler — runs once each time the player ship is touched by a enemy / hazard / bullet (see check_player_collisions). Plays OSWRCH 7 (the redefined BBC bell). Toggles lives_blink_state (0↔1) and DECs player_hp on every other call — so player_hp drops by 1 per actual death, with the blink toggle in between giving a two-frame red/yellow flash on the ship icon at the bottom of the screen. The icon is redrawn at column (player_hp + &1F), row &5F, using gfx_icon_03 (state 0) or the alternate icon at &48B8 (state 1). When player_hp hits 0 AND the blink-toggle says state 1, JMP death_anim (= explosion + DEC lives_left + init).
comments.0x1E46: Lives-icon blink-state — 0/1 toggle stepped by lose_a_life. Used both to swap the lives-icon sprite (gfx_icon_03 vs &48B8) and to halve the rate at which player_hp drops (DEC only on alternate calls).
comments.0x2050: Per-life HP — how many lose_a_life calls the current ship can absorb before it explodes. Reset to zp_9A by init (= zp_9A default 6, configurable from options.BAS as `?&9A = X%`). DEC'd in pairs by lose_a_life (every other call); when it hits 0 the next lose_a_life jumps to death_anim. Also used as the X-position of the lives icon in the bottom-status row (col player_hp + &1F).
comments.0x2051: Lives counter — number of ships remaining BEFORE the current one (i.e. continues / extra lives). Initialised by restore_score_from_loader from loader_lives_save (= 3 at game start; preserved across levels). DEC'd by death_anim after the explosion plays. main_loop polls this each game_step: when it reaches 0 → JMP game_over_or_continue.
comments.0x14D8: Force-pod animation frame (1 / 2 / 3). Selects which of the three gfx_pod_frame0/1/2 sprites draw_player_pod renders. Cycled by player vertical motion: each move_player_down INCs it (wraps 4→1), each move_player_up DECs it (wraps 0→3). Disk-initial value is 1.
comments.0x126C: Per-frame scroll entry. If zp_scroll_col has reached &F0 (the wrap point — last 240 columns of the map loop forever), restart the starfield and jump straight to update_hazards, skipping the scroll. Otherwise fall through to scroll_playfield.
comments.0x127B: Soft-scroll the playfield's upper and lower tile bands left by 8 pixels (one BBC char cell = 2 byte-cols), then plot the new column at the right edge (col 39). The inner copy loop is unrolled into two parallel pairs of self-modified `LDA &XXXX / STA &XXXX` instructions — one pair for the upper band (HI = &58 → screen row 0), one for the lower band (HI = &6C → screen row 16). The eight self-modified operand bytes are named `scroll_{upper,lower}_{src,dst}_{lo,hi}` and are incremented in tandem by the loop. Outer counter X=5 + inner loop runs 256 iterations each ⇒ 5×256 = 1280 bytes per band copied (= 4 char rows × 320 B/row, the full band). On exit, plot the freshly-scrolled-in column at (col &27, row 0) mirrored from zp_tile_upper, then (col &27, row 16) normal from zp_tile_lower.
comments.0x12BE: Inner body of scroll_playfield's copy loop. The four `LDA &FFFF / STA &FFFF` instructions use the literal &FFFF only as a placeholder — at runtime each instruction's operand bytes (the `scroll_{upper,lower}_{src,dst}_{lo,hi}` equates defined immediately above each LDA/STA via `* + 1` / `* + 2`) have been pre-set to a real screen address, then INC'd at the bottom of the loop. Each iteration's `LDA` reads one byte further right and the matching `STA` writes one byte further left, sliding the band leftward.
comments.0x210A: Per-frame hazard + force-field update + plot. First clears the force-field strip if forcefield_active=1 (erases a 2×&20 rectangle at (forcefield_x, forcefield_y) and clears the flag — the strip is procedural noise re-drawn every frame, so it has to be wiped before forcefield_render generates a fresh one). Then iterates the 8 hazard slots: for each active hazard (hazard_type != 0), DEC hazard_x to scroll it left, redraw, and dispatch any per-type behaviour via hazard_type_dispatch.
comments.0x208A: Spawn-table scanner — runs once per frame from main_loop. Compares lev_spawn_col[zp_7B] against zp_scroll_col; on match, decodes the paired lev_spawn_attr byte into a new hazard in the slot pool: bits 0..4 → hazard_type (0..31), bit 7 → hazard_flip (INVERTED: 0 in attr = upright, 1 = vertical-flip), bits 5..6 → spawn row 0..3 mapped to Y = &DF / &BF / &9F / &7F (4 evenly-spaced lines spanning the playfield gap between the upper and lower tile bands). Initial slot state: hazard_x = &28 (off-screen right, col 40), hazard_hp = &14 for type &10 / &06 for types &07 and < &03 / &08 otherwise, hazard_step = 4. Allocates slot via zp_7C (round-robin 0..7). On any non-spawning type (3..6 special-cased) it re-enters spawn_check_step to drain back-to-back spawns at the same column.
comments.0x22B2:
Hazard per-type IMMEDIATE action — called once per frame per active slot from update_hazard_slot, before frame-advance. Dispatch table by hazard_type[X]:
  type &04 → enqueue_hazard_bullet (hazard fires a leftward 12×2-px red bullet; pool at hazard_bullet_x/_y, 7 slots)
  type &06 → spawn_hazard_missile (CODE2: launch a vertical 4×6-px yellow-red rocket that horizontally homes on the player; pool at hazard_missile_x/_y, 2 slots)
  type &07 → forcefield_render (this slot IS a force-field — re-noise the strip every frame)
  type &08 → spawn_flame (start the one-shot 32×8-px flame projectile; auto-mutates to type 9 on success)
  type &13 → enqueue_hazard_bullet (same as type 4 — second hazard variant that fires a bullet)
  else     → RTS (no per-frame action; the slot just gets drawn and frame-advanced).
comments.0x2253: Per-frame action for one hazard slot (X = slot index). Two steps: (1) call hazard_type_dispatch for the per-type immediate action; if the dispatch path bumped hazard_type past &14 (the death-anim range), jump to hazard_death_step. (2) Otherwise gate frame-advance on zp_7F == 4 (the animation phase tick — zp_7F cycles 3/4 across frames, so anim only advances 50% of the time) and fall into hazard_anim_advance.
comments.0x2267:
Animation-frame state machine for one hazard slot — keyed on hazard_type[X]:
  type < &04 → anim_loop_1_to_3 (3-frame anim loop: 1→2→3→1, with type 4 hit briefly to spawn a bullet then immediately wrapped back to 1)
  type &08 or &09 → anim_toggle_8_9 (EOR #1 — flame-firing hazard alternates between pre-fire pose &08 and post-fire idle &09)
  type &0F → anim_set_type_10 (2-frame loop with &10)
  type &10 → anim_set_type_F (2-frame loop with &0F)
  &04..&07, &0A..&0E, &11..&13 → hazard_anim_done (no animation — these are either action types that mutate to their own next state on dispatch, or static decor)
  type ≥ &14 → hazard_death_step (the explosion-animation tail).
comments.0x2285: Small-hazard 3-frame animation loop (types 1, 2, 3). INC type; on hitting type 4 (which is the 'fire' marker, NOT a frame), wrap back to type 1. Note: type 4 IS reached momentarily — hazard_type_dispatch runs FIRST and enqueues a bullet on type 4, then this routine wraps the type back to 1.
comments.0x2295: Flame-firing hazard 2-state toggle. EOR #&01 flips between type &08 (about-to-fire pose; on the next dispatch call this triggers spawn_flame which itself bumps the slot to &09) and type &09 (post-fire idle).
comments.0x229B: Shared 'no animation' RTS — branches here from hazard_anim_advance for hazard types that don't auto-advance their frame (force-fields, enemies already-advanced by dispatch, idle states).
comments.0x229C: Explosion-animation step for a dying hazard. INC hazard_type so it walks &14 → &15 → ... → &1B (= 8 frames using lev_hazard_ptr_*[20..27]); on reaching &1C clears the slot completely (hazard_type/_x/_y = 0). Triggered when a hazard's HP hits 0 (check_bullet_hits sets hazard_type = &14 to start the death anim).
comments.0x22C3: Frame-advance for type-&0F hazards → set hazard_type = &10. Pairs with anim_set_type_F to form a 2-frame ping-pong (F↔10) for whichever hazard type this is (likely a 2-frame animated decor / persistent enemy with no death state).
comments.0x22C9: Frame-advance for type-&10 hazards → set hazard_type = &0F. The other half of the F↔10 ping-pong (see anim_set_type_10).
comments.0x22E5: Enqueue a hazard bullet — called from hazard_type_dispatch for hazard types &04 and &13. Scans hazard_bullet_x[6..1] for a &FF (free) slot; on found, stores `hazard_x[firer] - 1` at hazard_bullet_x[slot] and `hazard_y[firer] - &10` at hazard_bullet_y[slot] (Y lifted by 16 px to fire from above the hazard). Plays sfx_fire. If the firer is already at hazard_x < &08 (almost off-screen left) the bullet is suppressed — there's not enough screen left for it to fly. Per-frame mover: update_hazard_bullets. The bullet sprite is a 12×2-px red dart (3-byte-col × 2-line block at lev_erase_brush+&100); the deactivation erase brush is at +&102.
comments.0x1A99: Enemy 'try to fire a bullet' hook — called from update_enemies after plotting each active enemy slot. Random chance (rndval32 == 2 → 1/16) gates each call; further requires the enemy to be near the left edge (enemy_x < &10), the global cooldown zp_7E to be 0, and a free slot in hazard_bullet_x[4..1]. On success: stores enemy_x+2 / enemy_y-8 into the chosen slot, sets zp_7E = 8 (8-frame cooldown), plays sfx_hit_lo. Shares the hazard_bullet_x/_y pool with enqueue_hazard_bullet — enemies and hazards fire the same bullet sprite.
comments.0x1D35: Per-frame mover for the 6-slot hazard_bullet pool. For each active slot (hazard_bullet_x[X] != &FF): DEC X by 2 (move left 2 px), then either deactivate (X underflowed past &00, or new X <= 1) or re-plot the 12×2-px red dart at the new position. Each active slot then runs hazard_bullet_collide_player to test against the player ship and trigger lose_a_life on hit. Also DECs the global enemy-fire cooldown zp_7E.
comments.0x13D1: ONE COLUMN of game progress — called once per main_loop iteration. Does the per-tile-column setup (try_spawn_pickup, spawn_periodic_enemy, spawn_check_step, then computes the new right-edge tile sprite pointers `zp_tile_lower_hi/lo` and `zp_tile_upper_hi/lo` by indexing `lev_map_lower[zp_scroll_col]` / `lev_map_upper[zp_scroll_col]` into the tile catalog at &4E80, multiply-by-&80 via repeated ADC), bumps `zp_scroll_col` by 1 (with wrap from &F0+ back to &F0 = the end-of-map loop), checks the PAUSE key, then runs 4 sub-frames (`play_subframe` loop, gated on `zp_get_ready_erase` going 0→1→2→3→4). Each subframe scrolls the playfield by 4 px (so 4×4 = 16 px = one tile-cell per game_step call), updates hazards / enemies / missiles / pickups, draws score, reads input, and on even columns also bumps `zp_frame_progress` + draws the get_ready overlay. Returns early once `lives_left` drops to 0, which the caller `main_loop` immediately tests to go to game_over_or_continue.
comments.0x143C: Body of game_step's per-subframe inner loop. Runs once per BBC display frame (gated by frame_delay). 4 iterations are run per game_step (= per tile-column of horizontal scroll), producing the 4-frame soft-scroll animation between successive columns.
comments.0x1712: Periodic background-enemy spawner — called once per game_step. Doesn't run after the map has wrapped (zp_scroll_col == &F0). Otherwise paces on zp_87 (sub-counter, 0..A): when zp_87 reaches &0A, wait for the enemy field to fully empty, then advance `zp_86` (pattern selector, cycles 1..6 wrapping back to 1 after 7) and pre-load the per-pattern parameters from the LUTs at tbl_16FB / tbl_1702 / tbl_1709. On the other 10 calls of each cycle, INC zp_87 and spawn one enemy into the first free slot: enemy_x = &25 (right edge), enemy_state and enemy_y set per pattern (patterns 5/6: state 3/5, Y random in [&97..&D7]; others: state 1, Y = &9A fixed).
comments.0x25AA: Try to start a power-up pickup. Triggered when pickup_kill_count (the kill-milestone counter — INC'd in check_bullet_hits at enemy-hit values 3/5/7) reaches &0A. Requires no pickup already active (pickup_state == 0) and `player_missiles_unlocked != 1` (= the 7th-pickup ceiling — once the player has collected 7 power-ups, no more spawn). On success: stash player_y in pickup_y, set pickup_x = &27 (right edge col 39), pickup_state = 1 (animation start), play sfx_level_start.
comments.0x2693: Per-subframe power-up pickup updater. Returns immediately if pickup_state == 0. Otherwise calls L275F (collision test?), conditionally advances state, DECs pickup_x (drifts the pickup leftward 1 px per subframe), and on pickup_x == 0 deactivates and erases. While alive, plots one of two sprites (`gfx_pickup_yellow` or `gfx_pickup_red`) keyed on the current pickup_state value (state 2/6 = yellow, 3/5 = red, 4 = ?, others = erase) — gives the pickup a flashing animation as it crosses the playfield.
comments.0x2690: Power-up pickup X coordinate (column). DEC'd by 1 each subframe of game_step (so the pickup drifts leftward 16 px/sec = 4 px per game_step). When it hits 0, update_pickup deactivates and erases. Initial value &27 (right edge) on spawn from try_spawn_pickup.
comments.0x2691: Power-up pickup Y coordinate (scanline) — captured from player_y at the moment try_spawn_pickup ran, then held constant for the lifetime of the pickup.
comments.0x2692: Power-up pickup state: 0 = inactive (slot free for a new spawn). Non-zero = active, with the value selecting the sprite frame for the flashing animation (2/6 → gfx_pickup_yellow, 3/5 → gfx_pickup_red, others → erase) — wraps at &0A back to 1.
comments.0x1DA1: Box-collision test for one hazard_bullet against the player. Hit window: player_x in [bullet_x, bullet_x+5] AND player_y in [bullet_y, bullet_y+&10]. On hit: erase the 2×2 bullet trail with lev_erase_brush+&102, mark the slot inactive (&FF), and fall through into lose_a_life.
comments.0x23C5: Force-field current X — last column it was drawn at; used by L210A to erase before forcefield_render redraws a fresh strip.
comments.0x23C6: Force-field current Y — paired with forcefield_x.
comments.0x23C7: Force-field active flag: 1 = a force-field was drawn last frame and needs erasing this frame, 0 = clean. Set by forcefield_draw_or_erase, cleared by L210A's early erase branch (and by forcefield_erase if the field went off-screen).
comments.0x2464: Spawn the flame projectile. Called from hazard_type_dispatch when a hazard of type 8 wants to fire. Refuses if `flame_state != 0` (only one flame at a time) or if the firing hazard's `hazard_x < &0C` (it has already scrolled too far left to spawn from). On success: mutates the firing hazard to type 9 (the post-fire idle state), copies its position into `flame_x` / `flame_y` (with a per-flip Y offset), and sets `flame_state = 1` to start the 6-frame animation.
comments.0x249A: Flame projectile X coord — column. DEC'd by 1 per call to update_flame so the flame drifts left through the playfield as the scenery scrolls. When it falls below &02 the flame deactivates (off-screen left).
comments.0x249C: Flame state machine: 0 = inactive (slot free for a new spawn); 1..6 = animation frames (1=&3B00, 2=&3B40, 3=&3B80, then ping-pongs back 4=&3B80, 5=&3B40, 6=&3B00); 7 = deactivate next call. Each call to update_flame INCs the state (via advance_flame_state) or jumps straight to 7 if the player has been hit.
comments.0x249D: Per-frame flame update (called from update_hazards when `flame_state != 0`). Erases the previous 32×8 frame, shifts the flame left by 1 px (DEC flame_x), then either re-plots the next animation frame from `gfx_flame_frame0..2` (states 1..6) or deactivates (state 7 / off-screen). Each frame is 8 byte-cols × 8 lines = 32×8 px. There is only ever one flame on screen — `spawn_flame` checks `flame_state` and refuses to start a new one until this slot is free.
comments.0x24C4: Plot the flame at its updated position. Dispatches by `flame_state` to one of three frame plotters: state 1/6 → `plot_flame_frame_0` (`gfx_flame_frame0` at &3B00), state 2/5 → `plot_flame_frame_1` (`gfx_flame_frame1` at &3B40), state 3/4 → `plot_flame_frame_2` (`gfx_flame_frame2` at &3B80). State 7 → `deactivate_flame`.
comments.0x2517: Deactivate the flame — `flame_state = 0`. Reached when the flame has reached its final animation frame, drifts off the left edge, or has hit the player.
comments.0x2520: Box-test the flame against the player. Collision box is offset (+4, -4) from the plotted position, 6 px wide × &18 (24) lines tall. On hit: set `flame_state = 7` (so the next call deactivates) and JMP `lose_a_life`.
comments.0x2550: Tail of `flame_collide_player` for the no-hit case — INC `flame_state` so the next frame plots the next animation step (1→2→3→4→5→6→7→deactivate).
comments.0x1A55: Enemy slot pool — 11 bytes per array (slots 0..10), but only indices 1..n_enemies (1..8) are ever populated. Active per-slot state: enemy_x[i] / enemy_y[i] = screen position; enemy_state[i] = animation/lifecycle counter (0 = free, 1..8 = active, 9 = expiring, &0A = just hit / about to free). Plus enemy_pattern_step[i] (the byte-offset into the per-pattern motion script pointed at by zp_88) and enemy_pattern_id[i] (pattern selector 1..6, captured at spawn). Spawned by spawn_periodic_enemy with enemy_x = &25; per-frame stepped by update_enemies which calls L1B87 to walk each slot's script.
comments.0x1A76: Enemy per-slot pattern-step counter — byte-offset into the per-pattern motion script that update_enemies/L1B87 is currently walking. The script (pointed at by zp_88, length enemy_pattern_len bytes) is a sequence of (dy_dir, dx_dir) byte pairs. L1B87 reads `(zp_88),Y` at Y = enemy_pattern_step[X] into data_16F6 (vertical-dir) and `(zp_88),Y+1` into data_16F7 (horizontal-dir), then INYs twice; the next frame's L1B2D stashes the new Y back here. Wrap-around at enemy_pattern_len.
comments.0x1A81: Enemy per-slot pattern ID — selects which motion pattern the slot follows. Set at spawn by spawn_periodic_enemy (cycles 1..6 via zp_86). Values 5 and 6 are special-cased in L1B87 (player-homing: pattern 5 toggles horizontal dir based on player_x; pattern 6 = straight at the player). Other values walk the table at (zp_88) verbatim. Slot 0 unused, slots 1..n_enemies (1..8) active; only 10 bytes wide (vs 11 for the position/state arrays) because enemy_pattern_id slot 10 wasn't allocated.
comments.0x1577: Player-missile launch (called from on_fire_pressed). Gated on `player_missiles_unlocked == 1` (= the player has collected 7 pickups → tier 7) AND `player_missile_step == 0` (= no pair currently in flight). On launch: captures the player position into two parallel slots — upper (player_missile_x[0], player_missile_y[0] = player_x, player_y + 2) and lower (player_missile_x[1], player_missile_y[1] = player_x, player_y - &12). Activates both slots (player_missile_active = {1, 1}), sets sprite-frame = 1 and step counter = 1. Suppresses the upper missile if player_y >= &D8 (too low — would be off-screen) and suppresses the lower if player_y < &A0 (too high). On the next pass, `update_player_missiles` (called per-subframe) drives the pair.
comments.0x15C4: Per-subframe ticker for the paired player missiles. RTS if `player_missile_step == 0` (idle). Phase 0: steps 1..4 spread the pair vertically (INC upper_y, DEC lower_y); phase 1: steps 5..7 advance the missile-frame index 1→2→3→4→5→2 (ping-pong loop) and walk the pair rightward by +1 px per subframe; phase 2: steps 8+ accelerate to +2 px/subframe. Each subframe re-plots whichever slots are active via `plot_player_missile`. When player_missile_x[0] >= &24 (off the right edge), erases both sprites with lev_erase_brush+&80 and resets the state (step = 0, x = 0). Note that the per-slot `player_missile_active` flag is cleared independently by `missile_hit_hazard` when a missile connects.
comments.0x1689: Plot one player-missile frame at the screen position already set up by the caller's calc_screen_addr. Selects the sprite via the inline multiply: `LDA #0 / LDX player_missile_frame / loop ADC #&28 / DEX / BNE / SBC #&28 / STA src_lo / LDA #&44 / STA src_hi` — so frame N (1..5) → src = &4400 + (N-1)*&28 = &4400/&4428/&4450/&4478/&44A0. Always 5×8 px. (Note: the existing GRAPHIX cfg labels gfx_missile_0..4 at &4410..&44B0 are inconsistent with this — the actual frames L1689 reads are 16 bytes lower in memory. Either the GRAPHIX labels are off by one, or this routine has a constant-offset bug producing scrambled-looking trails.)
comments.0x23C8: Per-frame test of both player missiles against the current hazard slot (X = hazard slot index, passed in via data_16F5). Sets up zp_test_x = upper_x + 3 and zp_test_y = upper_y - 4, calls `missile_hazard_box_test` with Y = 0 if the upper slot is active; then repeats with the lower slot's Y (zp_test_y = lower_y - 4) and Y = 1. Called from update_hazards' per-slot tail (L21AC, L21EB).
comments.0x23F9: Box-test the hazard at slot X (data_16F5) against the test point (zp_test_x, zp_test_y). Hit window: hazard_x in [test_x - 5, test_x] AND hazard_y in [test_y, test_y + &20]. On hit → `missile_hit_hazard`. Y on entry encodes which missile is being tested (0 = upper, 1 = lower) and is used by the hit path to deactivate the right slot and pick the right Y coordinate.
comments.0x241A: Player missile hits hazard. Plays sfx_explode; uses the Y passed in (0 = upper, 1 = lower) to deactivate the corresponding `player_missile_active[Y]` and erase the corresponding sprite at (player_missile_x[Y], player_missile_y[Y]). Then DECs hazard_hp[X] by 4 — on kill (hp == 0) jumps to L2458 (the hazard-death path); on damage-but-not-killed RTSes.
comments.0x1BE3: Enemy sprite-frame dispatcher (X = data_16F5 = current enemy slot). For enemy_state in 0..6 it indexes into the parallel `enemy_anim_ptr_lo` / `enemy_anim_ptr_hi` LUT: slots 1..4 → `lev_enemy_0..3` (LEVD1), slots 5..6 → `gfx_enemy_small_frame0/1` (GRAPHIX, two 4×24 winged-creature frames). For state >= &0A it dispatches to one of the hit-animation routines: &0A → L1C1A (sprite_src = &4C20 = lev_enemy_hit_1), &0B → L1C2B (lev_enemy_hit_2), &0C → L1C3C (lev_enemy_hit_3), &0D → L1C4D (final). Sets sprite_src_lo/hi for the caller to plot.
comments.0x25A1: 8-element pickup tier-flag array (&25A1..&25A8), indexed by `pickup_count - 1`. Set by `set_pickup_tier_flag` to mark each tier as reached. Three slots overlap with named flags whose meaning is consumed elsewhere: tier 3 (offset +2) = `pod_attached` (pod sprite + pod_collide_enemy kicks in), tier 5 (offset +4) = `force_pod_state` (pod_fire enabled + force_pod_anim runs), tier 7 (offset +6) = `player_missiles_unlocked` (`fire_player_missiles` enabled — a paired upper+lower 5×8 missile pattern fires on the next fire-button press; the spawn-block side-effect just falls out — there's nothing left to upgrade). Tiers 2, 4, 6, 8 (offsets +1, +3, +5, +7) ARE written but nothing reads them — orphan flags, presumably scaffolding for power-ups that were planned but never implemented (no consumer code).
comments.0x275F: Per-subframe pickup ↔ player collision test + tier ladder. Box-test the player against (pickup_x, pickup_y) with a (6 × &1C) hit window; miss → RTS. On hit: clear pickup_state, erase the pickup sprite (3×&10 brush), play sfx_level_start, add 100 to score (add_score_x10 with zp_score_acc=10? — TODO confirm), INC pickup_count, plot the +1 icon on the status row, then dispatch to `apply_pickup_tier`. Effects ladder (cumulative per game/life): #1 → fast fire (fire_cooldown_reload = 3); #2 → pickup_tier_flag+1 (unused); #3 → pod_attached = 1; #4 → unused; #5 → force_pod_state = 1 AND reset fire timer zp_8A = 8; #6 → unused; #7 → player_missiles_unlocked = 1; #8+ → no effect (count clamped). Called from update_pickup every active subframe.
comments.0x27D2: Tier-flag dispatcher. X = pickup_count, decrement to get the tier index (0-based), then jump to `set_pickup_tier_flag` if X < 7. Falls through to RTS for tier indices >= 7 (i.e. pickup_count >= 8).
comments.0x27D9: Set the per-tier pickup flag at `pickup_tier_flag,X`. The interesting tiers are X=2 (= pod_attached), X=4 (= force_pod_state, also resets zp_8A), X=6 (= player_missiles_unlocked). Tiers X=1/3/5/7 write to dead slots.
comments.0x2555: Per-frame pod ↔ enemy collision check (X = enemy slot). Only runs when the force-pod power-up is attached to the player (`pod_attached == 1`) and the enemy slot is active (`enemy_state < 9`). Test box is 3×&14 px at (player_x+8, player_y-11) — matches where draw_player_pod plots the pod sprite (offset (+5, +1) from the player). On hit: marks the slot &0A (expired), plays OSWRCH &07 (collision bell), runs `add_score_x1` for the score, and INCs `pickup_kill_count` (the kill-milestone counter that triggers a pickup spawn every 10 hits). Differs from `check_player_collisions` in that this is the POD doing damage to the enemy (scoring), whereas check_player_collisions is the enemy damaging the player (lose_a_life). Called from update_enemies per-slot.
comments.0x25D7: Score accumulator: ADD `zp_score_acc` to the units digit (`score + 4`) and propagate the carry chain through the 4 display digits (score+3 → +2 → +1 → +0). The score buffer is 6 bytes MSD-first at `score..score + 5`: bytes 0..3 are the four visible display digits, byte 4 is the actual units (which is what gets incremented), byte 5 is always 0 (the trailing '0' shown on screen so a score of 1230 stores as {0,1,2,3,0,0} and a +10 award stores `zp_score_acc = 1` then calls `add_score_x10`). Carries cascade through `add_score_carry_x{1,10,100,1000}`.
comments.0x25E5: Score accumulator x10: ADD `zp_score_acc` to the tens digit (`score + 3`) and carry-chain through score+2 → +1 → +0. Equivalent to `add_score_x1` with the accumulator pre-multiplied by 10. The fall-through code at &25EE..&2618 (extra ADC blocks for score+2/+1/+0) is unreachable — RTS after each carry check exits before the next block, and the routine instead jumps straight into the shared `add_score_carry_*` chain via the BCS branches.
comments.0x2619: Score carry (units → tens): subtract 10 from the overflowed units digit, INC the tens digit (score+3), and if THAT now equals 10 fall into `add_score_carry_x10` to propagate. Reached from `add_score_x1` when score+4 wraps past 10.
comments.0x262A: Score carry (tens → hundreds): subtract 10 from score+3, INC score+2 (hundreds), recurse if it also wraps. Shared by `add_score_x1` and `add_score_x10`.
comments.0x263B: Score carry (hundreds → thousands): subtract 10 from score+2, INC score+1 (thousands), recurse if it also wraps.
comments.0x264C: Score carry (thousands → ten-thousands): subtract 10 from score+1, INC score (the MSD = ten-thousands). No further carry — overflow past 99,999,0 silently wraps.
comments.0x13BC: Frame-rate regulator (busy-wait). Called once per main_loop iteration. zp_frame_progress accumulates 'work done this frame' (e.g. update_bullets does `INC zp_frame_progress` twice per active bullet, get_ready_overlay bumps it once per other frame). At entry, zp_frame_progress starts at zp_game_speed (set by the user's Speed option in `options.BAS` line 700: `?&92 = 120 + S%*30`, so 120 = slow .. 240 = fast; the BREAK cheat in Loader2 line 1310 sets it to 252). The busy loop runs (256 - X) outer iterations × 256-cycle inner loop, so the delay is INVERSELY proportional to zp_frame_progress: heavy frames (high X) get short delays, idle frames (X near zp_game_speed) get long delays — total frame time is roughly constant. The `CPX #&64 / BCS / LDX #&FE` guard handles the overflow case where so much work happened that zp_frame_progress wrapped past 255 to a small value (< 100): substitute X=&FE for a fixed minimum delay (~2 outer iterations) rather than the paradoxically-long (256-X) the underflowed value would imply. After the wait, zp_frame_progress is reset to zp_game_speed for the next frame.
